subject: Biology
title: Plant Hormones
description: A short lesson to help you apply Plant Hormones.

hook.text: Plants can't move toward sunlight. Instead, they grow toward it—guided by chemical hormones.

buildup.text: Auxin is a hormone that controls cell elongation. It makes stems bend toward light.

discovery.text: Gibberellins trigger seed germination and stem growth. Ethylene ripens fruit.

twist.text: One rotten apple really does spoil the bunch—it releases ethylene gas that ripens nearby fruit.

climax.text: Plants use hormones to coordinate growth, defense, flowering, and response to stress.

punchline.text: Plants can't run, so they grow their way out of trouble.

quiz.question: Which hormone makes plant stems bend toward light?
